course_title: Introduction to Professional Skills in Occupational Therapy
course_description:
This course will develop an understanding of, and skills in, therapeutic and professional communication, the therapeutic use of self, interviewing, and reflective practice.

In OCTY1102 you will commence your journey towards becoming a health professional. Professionalism is important for the quality of health care, including your future clients’ safety and health outcomes, and the nature of relationships between health care professionals.

To become a professional occupational therapist, you need to understand the ethical and moral principals which guide practice, and your responsibility to uphold these. You also need to develop specific behaviours and competencies.

This course will develop your self-awareness on how you are travelling on your journey towards professionalism, by introducing you to the process of reflective practice.

The course focusses on developing skills in therapeutic and professional communication. You will learn about ‘therapeutic use of self’, and how to intentionally use specific techniques to communicate in a manner which is highly responsive to the diverse needs and preferences of all communication partners. Communication skills are applied in tutorials and assessment in the context of conducting an interview, however, these skills are relevant to all professional interactions, relationships, and the therapy process.
timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
course_aims: This course focuses on cultivating the professional skills required to practice as an occupational therapist. Emphasis is placed on the developing an understanding of personal and professional values, building therapeutic and inclusive communication skills,ﾠtherapeutic use of self, interviewing skills, and reflective practice.ﾠStudents will have numerous opportunities to practice their therapeutic communication and interviewing skills in both small groups and 1:1 situations.ﾠ

learning_outcomes.description: Coursework - Identify personal values, beliefs, life experiences and interpersonal characteristics that impact on your development as a health professional (including communication in various contexts), showing self-awareness.
Coursework - Demonstrate the ability to engage in insightful self- reflection regarding professional development (e.g., of personal communication skills).
Coursework - Apply your understanding of interviewing and communication skills to generate, deliver and reflectively respond to peer feedback.
Coursework - Demonstrate verbal and non-verbal therapeutic communication skills including listening, questioning, and assertiveness in 1:1 and group situations.
Coursework - Identify responsive strategies to meet the diverse needs and preferences of all communication partners.
Coursework - Explain the therapeutic (intentional) use of self and the application of these principles to OT practice.

assessment_details.title: In-semester Therapeutic Communication Exam
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This in-semester exam is scheduled on Monday in Week 7, earlier than (and in lieu of) the usual Monday lecture (the exam has a 15:00 start time). Content from lectures, tutorials and required readings from Weeks 1-6 inclusive is examinable. It is not an open-book exam. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Deferred exams can be requested for this assessment item. Please apply for a deferred exam if there are exceptional or unavoidable circumstances, or as a one-off discretionary request. Deferred exam requests should be submitted as soon as possible, and no later than five calendar days after the date of the original exam. Further details, including how to apply, can be found at - Deferring an exam . Please also email the Course Coordinator to advise of the deferred exam request. assessment_details.title: Tutorial Reflections
assessment_details.category: Participation/ Student contribution, Practical/ Demonstration, Reflection, Role play/ Simulation
assessment_details.due_date: Complete weekly tutorial activities in weeks 1 - 10 and submit these during your tutorial from 23/02/2026 - 4/05/2026 Complete a tutorial activity and submit during your week 12 tutorial from 18/05/2026
assessment_details.task_description: Students will be split into four (4) tutorial groups. You will be assigned to one (1) weekly tutorial group. 11 tutorials during the semester (weeks 1-10, and 12) focus on development of core professional skills. To facilitate development of reflective practice, three questions are to be completed in the final 10 minutes of these tutorials. These reflections will be submitted at the end of the tutorial. Each reflection will be worth 1.5 marks and the best 8 will contribute to the total mark (out of 12). During the tutorial in week 11, students will participate in a practice for their Practical Exam (Final Interview). There will be no ‘tutorial reflection’ submitted during this tutorial, however students will provide a peer with a copy of a completed Peer Interview Feedback Form, which is critical to completion of the Reflection Report assessment, and should be submitted with the Reflection Report assignment. The tutorial in week 13 is dedicated to supporting preparation for the final two pieces of assessment, and no tutorial reflection will be submitted. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) This assessment is to be completed in person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Tutorial Reflections will be submitted during the tutorial in hard copy.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
To account for missed tutorials, only the best 8 of 11 submissions count towards the final mark.
If you anticipate missing more than three of the 11 tutorials when reflections are submitted (weeks 1-10, and 12), you may submit an extension request.

assessment_details.title: Reflection Report
assessment_details.task_description: Reflective practice is the process of reflecting on an experience or activity and underpins occupational therapy practice. In this assessment you are required to apply a framework for reflection, identifying the strengths and weaknesses in your communication skills, and discerning what you need to focus on to enhance communication in your end-of-semester final interview assessment interview and with your clients in the future. The Reflection Report is based upon an activity completed in the tutorial in week 11 of semester, when pairs of students will practice for the Final Interview Assessment, taking it in turns to: 1) interview/be interviewed, and 2) provide each other verbal and written peer feedback using the Peer Interview Feedback Form (PIFF). As both you and your peers need to experience this practice interview activity and feedback process to write a Reflection Report, attendance at the week 11 tutorial is strongly encouraged. Students who do not attend the week 11 tutorial will need to coordinate with each other to complete the activity and PIFF outside of class time in a timely manner so that neither you, nor peers, are delayed in commencing work on the Reflection Report. Attendance at the week 11 tutorial will be recorded to track students' completion of the interview practice activity and feedback process. The Peer Interview Feedback Form that you completed on your partner during the practice interview in week 11 must be submitted via Turnitin on Learn.UQ when you submit your Reflection Report Assessment. Refer to the task description and marking rubric that will be in the Assessment Folder on Learn.UQ for further details. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The reflection report is to be submitted online via Turnitin on Learn.UQ.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Extensions can be requested for this assessment item. Please apply for an extension request via my.UQ > my.UQ Dashboard > my. Requests and provide evidence of circumstance, as soon as it becomes evident that an extension is needed, but no later than the assessment item submission date. Further information regarding what constitutes an acceptable reason for an extension is listed on the my.UQ website: Applying for an extension - my.UQ - University of Queensland

assessment_details.title: Practical Exam - Final Interview
assessment_details.category: Examination, Practical/ Demonstration, Role play/ Simulation
assessment_details.due_date: End of Semester Exam Period 6/06/2026 - 20/06/2026 End of Semester Exam Period
assessment_details.task_description: Students will undertake an interview with a peer during the end-of-semester exam block. The interviews will audio-recorded for moderating purposes, and assessed in-person by a marker on the day. The peer whom you will interview will be decided by assessors on the day of the interview just prior to the interview. Refer to the task description and marking rubric that will be in the Assessment Folder on Learn.UQ for further details. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Recording of assessment When a student is required to complete an oral or practical assessment item worth 20% or more, the performance will be recorded and retained per Appendix 1. learning_resources.library_resources: Find the required and recommended resources for this course on the UQ Library website.

learning_activities.topic: Optional skill development workshop An optional workshop for further practice of the communication skills learned in class time will be offered in approximatly week 10 of semester (date and time to be confirmed)

Online independent learning activities Students will be informed of online resources relevant to each lecture/tutorial, and are expected to engage independently with the required readings. Recommended readings are also nominated. These: are provided to support students who wish to review a topic covered in class or extend their understanding of a topic. will not be directly assessed in the in-semester exam. Online training modules have been designed to complement other learning activites and provide additional opportunities for practicing core communication skills in some weeks of the semester. These are not compulsory. Each module takes approximately 15 minutes. Learning outcomes: L01, L04, L05, L06

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (23 Feb - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic: Lectures Weekly interactive lectures are provided on Monday afternoons 16:00 - 17:50. A detailed schedule of lectures will be provided on Learn.UQ. Lectures will be recorded and made available on Learn.UQ, however in-person attendance is recommended to benefit from the interactive elements, and fully engage with the new content fully prior to the Tuesday morning tutorials. In week 7 there is no lecture as the in-semester exam is scheduled 15:00 - 17:30. In week 10 there is no live lecture due to the Monday public holiday. Students will have access to a pre-recorded lecture to watch in their own time this week. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (23 Feb - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic: Tutorials Tutorials are in-person, last 1.5 hours and will not be recorded. These sessions are designed as experiential learning activities, and participants are expected to attend and actively engage. Students will reflect upon the learning activities individually and with peers. This is a time for practice and refinement of specific skills in small groups. A detailed schedule of tutorial topics will be provided on Learn.UQ. During the final 10 minutes of 11 of the 13 tutorials, students will be guided to compile a brief written reflection on an element of the tutorial. These reflections form the ‘Tutorial Reflections’ described in the Assessment section of the ECP, and more detail is provided in the assessment task description on Learn.UQ. The week 11 tutorial provides a particular practical experience, preparing students for the final interview assessment in the end of semester exam period. In this tutorial students will also provide a peer with feedback on their interview skills. Each student needs to give and receive peer feedback as this forms the basis of the Reflection Report assessment. The week 13 tutorial will provide support in preparing for the final two pieces of assessment (Reflection Report and Final Interview). Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
